description_long: This object is a weathered stone cross grave marker, standing upright and partially surrounded by patches of green grass and earth. The cross is made of rough, light gray stone, with visible chisel marks and some surface erosion, giving it an aged appearance. The front face of the cross features faint, hand-carved inscriptions, though some of the text is worn and difficult to read. The base of the cross is embedded in the ground, with tufts of grass and soil encroaching around its lower section. The crossbar is slightly uneven, and the overall structure appears solid and heavy. The surrounding vegetation and earth suggest it is located in an outdoor graveyard or cemetery setting.
description: A weathered stone cross grave marker with faint inscriptions, surrounded by grass and earth, showing signs of age and erosion.
description_view_0: Front view showing the inscribed face of the stone cross, with visible text and grass at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the cross and the depth of the stone, with grass and soil at the base.
description_view_2: Rear view of the cross, showing a plain, uninscribed stone surface and more surrounding vegetation.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, displaying the cross's profile and the encroaching grass and earth at the base.
